title: Malache arachnoidea
intro: Malache arachnoidea, also known as Spun Gland Spider Wort or simply Spider Wort, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its unique, spider-like inflorescences. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including skin problems, wounds, and infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Malache arachnoidea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Spider Wort is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture, often used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis, as well as to promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Malache arachnoidea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the genus Malache, which belongs to the family Malaceae. It is a part of the order Rosales, a group of flowering plants that are primarily woody and have simple or compound leaves. The family Malaceae is a small group of plants that are native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world.
